User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account in the email server system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

isVerified.description: Indicates whether the user's email has been verified.

Domain.title: Domain
Domain.description: Represents a domain managed by the email server system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the domain entity.

verificationStatus.description: Status of the domain verification process (pending, verified, failed).

EmailAccount.title: EmailAccount
EmailAccount.description: Represents an email account within a managed domain.

id.description: Unique identifier for the email account entity.

emailAddress.description: The email address of the account.

storageQuota.description: Storage quota allocated to the email account (in MB).

EmailMessage.title: EmailMessage
EmailMessage.description: Represents an email message stored in the system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the email message entity.

sentAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the email was sent.

id.description: Unique identifier for the activity log entry.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of the activity log entry.

definition.description: Stores user account information. Path-based ownership for private user data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores domain information for a specific user. Enforces ownership through the path. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: domainId
params.description: The unique identifier for the domain.

definition.description: Stores email account information within a domain. Inherits ownership from the user and domain. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'domainId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: domainId
params.description: The unique identifier for the domain.

params.name: emailAccountId
params.description: The unique identifier for the email account.

definition.description: Stores email messages for a specific email account. Inherits ownership from the user, domain, and email account. Includes denormalized 'userId', 'domainId', and 'emailAccountId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: domainId
params.description: The unique identifier for the domain.

params.name: emailAccountId
params.description: The unique identifier for the email account.

params.name: emailMessageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the email message.

definition.description: Stores activity logs for a specific user. Enforces ownership through the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: activityLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for the activity log.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support the email server management system, NubMail, focusing on secure and scalable data management.  Authorization Independence is achieved by using path-based ownership for user-specific data and membership maps for collaborative data. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules.  Structural Segregation ensures that each collection has a homogeneous security posture.  Access Modeling employs consistent patterns for authorization, with path-based ownership for User-owned data and membership maps where collaboration is needed.  The design supports QAPs through structural segregation, enabling secure `list` operations.

*   User data is stored under `/users/{userId}`, ensuring that each user's data is isolated and access is controlled based on the authenticated user ID.
*   Domains are stored as subcollections of users (`/users/{userId}/domains/{domainId}`), enforcing ownership and simplifying access control.
*   Email accounts are nested under domains (`/users/{userId}/domains/{domainId}/emailAccounts/{emailAccountId}`), maintaining the hierarchical relationship and owner context.
*   Email messages are stored within email accounts (`/users/{userId}/domains/{domainId}/emailAccounts/{emailAccountId}/emailMessages/{emailMessageId}`), creating a clear and secure structure for email data.
*   Activity logs are kept at the user level (`/users/{userId}/activityLogs/{activityLogId}`), allowing for easy auditing and monitoring of user actions.

This structure guarantees that security rules can be written clearly and efficiently, without relying on complex data relationships or server-side filtering.
